subject: Mathematics
title: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.text: The pair of equations y = 0 and y = -7 has:
questions.explanation: y = 0 is the x-axis and y = -7 is a line parallel to the x-axis. Since they are parallel, they never intersect, resulting in no solution.

questions.text: If the lines given by 3x + 2ky = 2 and 2x + 5y + 1 = 0 are parallel, then the value of k is:
questions.explanation: For parallel lines, a1/a2 = b1/b2. So, 3/2 = 2k/5. Cross-multiplying gives 15 = 4k, hence k = 15/4.

questions.text: A pair of linear equations which has a unique solution x = 2, y = -3 is:
questions.explanation: Substituting x=2 and y=-3 into x+y=-1 gives 2-3=-1 (True). For 2x-3y=13, it gives 2(2)-3(-3) = 4+9=13 (True).

questions.text: If a1/a2 = b1/b2 = c1/c2, then the lines are:
questions.explanation: When all ratios are equal, the equations are dependent and the lines coincide, meaning they have infinitely many solutions.

questions.text: The sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 9. If 27 is added to it, the digits of the number get reversed. The number is:
questions.explanation: 36: 3+6=9. Adding 27 to 36 gives 63, which is the reverse of 36.
